title: The Macro Minute: What is the status of the US and global economies?

Happy Thursday out there team 42 or
skipper here at Darius Delta to present
our macro minute for Thursday October
9th 2025. As always we'll start at the
executive summary from today's leadoff
morning note. If you would like to
review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk
or to get on the right side of market
risk to the extent you haven't been
following KISS or Dr. Mo, you need to be
a client of 42 macro. So today's macro
question is what is the status of the US
and global economies? Our answer, the US
government may still be shut down, but
our team at 42 macro is still hard at
work for team 42. There are dozens of
key high frequency economic statistics
released around the world that provided
clues regarding the likely persistence
of the current risk on vodilocks market
regime. Unpacking the data, the
September New York Fed survey of
consumer expectations generally
supported our outlook for firming growth
and firming inflation in the US economy,
while the September New York Fed
services sector survey did not support
our outlook for firming growth, but did
support our outlook for firming
inflation. In the Euro zone, the August
real retail sales data supported our
outlook for slowing growth, and we
currently project firming inflation amid
a der of key high frequency inflation
indicators since our last update. In
Japan, the August household spending,
September economy watcher survey, and
September machine tool or machine tool
orders data do not support our outlook
for moderating growth, while the August
wage data supported our outlook for
slowing inflation. Perhaps most
importantly, our grid model is currently
projecting growth to beat consensus
expectations throughout our next 12
month forecast horizons in the US,
Brazil, China, Euro zone, France, India,
and Japan.
This persistent upside surprise risk in
the US and global macro and micro data
remains a core feature of the 100
percentile bullish paradigm C theme we
authored back in April. Investor
consensus is bullish according to our
qualitative research process and
quantitative research process but they
are clearly not bullish enough relative
to the historically expansive scope of
paradigm C. Refer to our September 24th
leadoff morning note in which we asked
and answered is the stock market about
to bubble for more details. Transition
to our 42 macro dashboard. As always, we
wrap up with a question from our
community. This one's not really a
question, but a very important comment
uh titled kissing your retirement. So,
it reads, I often hear the KISS
portfolio is described as a strategy to
help you retire on time and comfortable.
As someone who has already done just
that, I'd like to offer another
perspective. Kiss isn't just for getting
to retirement. It's also an excellent
approach for staying secure in
retirement. The goal of investing once
retired to protect against left tail
risk without giving up meaningful
upside. Bonds used to play that role,
but in today's environment, they become
more of a drag than a shield. What KISS
provides is a smart, disciplined
framework that reduces downside risk
while still allowing for real growth. In
the past two years with KISS, I've
experienced the best returns of my
investing life without the stress and
sleepless nights that often come with
managing retirement savings. That peace
of mind is priceless. And the only thing
that might make it even better is when
you launch your ETF. Thank you, Darius
and team 42. So, thank you for the
really uh kind compliment. Um, this is
why we built the company. This right
here is exactly why we built the
company, why we built KISS, is to help
thousands, perhaps tens, if not hundreds
of thousands of investors around the
world over time retire on time and
comfortably and sleep comfortably at
night knowing that they have the peace
of mind provided by institutional grade
quantitative versus management overlays
that aren't being used to charge people
2 and 20 or 3 and 30, but are being used
to help ordinary main street investors
do exactly what we, the people in the
financial services industry, should be
doing uh to help folks like this. So,
just want to say thank you for the kind
compliment and uh best of luck out there
everyone. We'll catch you back here on
Monday. Have a great weekend. Cheers.
